program_outcomes: Matematik, fen bilimleri ve bilgisayar mühendisliğine özgü konularda yeterli bilgi birikimi; bu alanlardaki kuramsal ve uygulamalı bilgileri, karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinde kullanabilme becerisi.
Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerini saptama, tanımlama, formüle etme ve çözme becerisi; bu amaçla uygun analiz ve modelleme yöntemlerini seçme ve uygulama becerisi.

Karmaşık bir sistemi, süreci, cihazı veya ürünü gerçekçi kısıtlar ve koşullar altında, belirli gereksinimleri karşılayacak şekilde tasarlama becerisi; bu amaçla modern tasarım yöntemlerini uygulama ve gerçekleştirim becerisi.

Mühendislik uygulamalarında karşılaşılan karmaşık problemlerin analizi ve çözümü için gerekli olan modern teknik ve araçları geliştirme, seçme ve kullanma becerisi; bilişim teknolojilerini etkin bir şekilde kullanma becerisi.

Karmaşık mühendislik problemlerinin veya bilgisayar mühendisliğine özgü araştırma konularının incelenmesi için deney tasarlama, deney yapma, veri toplama, sonuçları analiz etme ve yorumlama becerisi.
Disiplin içi ve çok disiplinli takımlarda etkin biçimde çalışabilme becerisi; bireysel çalışma becerisi.

Türkçe sözlü ve yazılı etkin iletişim kurma becerisi; en az bir yabancı dil bilgisi; etkin rapor yazma ve yazılı raporları anlama, tasarım ve üretim raporları hazırlayabilme, etkin sunum yapabilme, açık ve anlaşılır talimat verme ve alma becerisi.
Yaşam boyu öğrenmenin gerekliliği bilinci; bilgiye erişebilme, bilim ve teknolojideki gelişmeleri izleme ve kendini sürekli yenileme becerisi.
Etik ilkelerine uygun davranma, mesleki ve etik sorumluluk bilinci; mühendislik uygulamalarında kullanılan standartlar hakkında bilgi.

Proje yönetimi, risk yönetimi ve değişiklik yönetimi gibi, iş hayatındaki uygulamalar hakkında bilgi; girişimcilik, yenilikçilik hakkında farkındalık; sürdürülebilir kalkınma hakkında bilgi.

Mühendislik uygulamalarının evrensel ve toplumsal boyutlarda sağlık, çevre ve güvenlik üzerindeki etkileri ve çağın mühendislik alanına yansıyan sorunları hakkında bilgi; mühendislik çözümlerinin hukuksal sonuçları konusunda farkındalık.
Karmaşık bilgisayar mühendisliği problemlerinin çözümü için olasılık, istatistik ve ayrık matematik bilgisi.

courses.name: INTRODUCTION TO PROGRAMMING I
courses.purpose: This course serves as an introduction to the fundamentals of computer science and programming. It aims to help students with little or no programming experience to gain necessary skills to work with abstract notions for solving computational problems.
courses.description: The course is structured around basic topics such as control flow, functions, lists, file I/O, simple data structures, function design, recursion and memory management. The class will use the Python and C programming languages as medium to provide basic understanding of concepts in computer programming.
learning_outcomes: At the end of this course successful students will understand the basic principles of computer programming and algorithmic problem solving, and will be able to develop algorithms and to write computer programs of moderate complexity.
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Binary representations and the Von Neumann architecture
Hafta 3: Introduction to Python, Control flow, Functions
Hafta 4: Tuples, Lists, File IO, Sets, Dictionaries
Hafta 10: Development Strategies, Function Design, Algorithmic Speed
resources: Think Python, 2nd edition, Allen B. Downey
Introduction to Computation and Programming Using Python, 2nd edition, John V. Guttag, MIT Press, 2013
C How to Program (8th Edition), Paul D. Deitel, Harvey Deitel, Pearson, 2016.
The C Programming Language, 2nd Edition, Brian Kernighan and Dennis Ritchie, Prentice Hall, 1988
Practical C Programming 3rd Edition, Steve Oualline, O'Reilly Media, 1997

courses.name: Introduction to Programming Laboratory I
courses.purpose: This course serves as an introduction to the fundamentals of computer science and programming. It aims to help students with little or no programming experience to gain necessary skills to work with abstract notions for solving computational problems.
courses.description: The course is structured around basic topics such as control flow, functions, lists, file I/O, simple data structures, function design, recursion and memory management. The class will use the Python and C programming languages as medium to provide basic understanding of concepts in computer programming.
learning_outcomes: At the end of this course successful students will understand the basic principles of computer programming and algorithmic problem solving, and will be able to develop algorithms and to write computer programs of moderate complexity.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Introduction to Computer Programming with C, Sequential Structure
Hafta 5: Data types, Variables and Operators
resources: Think Python, 2nd edition, Allen B. Downey
Introduction to Computation and Programming Using Python, 2nd edition, John V. Guttag, MIT Press, 2013
C How to Program (8th Edition), Paul J. Deitel, Harvey Deitel, Pearson, 2016.
The C Programming Language, 2nd Edition, Brian Kernighan and Dennis Ritchie, Prentice Hall, 1988
Practical C Programming 3rd Edition, Steve Oualline, O'Reilly Media, 1997

courses.name: Introduction to Computer Engineering
courses.purpose: To familiarize students with core concepts related to Computer Engineering, the B.Sc. Computer Engineering curriculum, the department's ICT infrastructure, contemporary issues and research directions in Computer Engineering, and ethical and legal issues in programming and computer science research.
courses.description: An orientation course that provides guidance to students about the Department and Computer Engineering in general. It introduces ethical and legal issues related to computer engineering and presents the faculty and their activities.
learning_outcomes: Have knowledge about basic subjects and research areas of Computer Engineering.
Indicate the curriculum, the faculty and the research activities of the department.
Identify and use the facilities of the department.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to the course, Review of Department's educational system
Hafta 2: Introduction to Computer Science: The role and science of algorithms, abstraction
Hafta 3: Algorithms: The Concept of an Algorithm, Algorithm Representation, Algorithm Discovery, Iterative Structures, Recursive Structures, Efficiency and Correctness
Hafta 4: Data Storage: Bits and Their Storage, Main Memory, Mass Storage, Representing Information as Bit Patterns, The Binary System, Data Compression, Communications Errors.
Hafta 5: Data Manipulation: Computer Architecture, Machine Language, Program Execution.
Hafta 6: Operating Systems: The History of Operating Systems, Operating System Architecture, Coordinating the Machine's Activities, Handling Competition Among Processes
Hafta 8: Networking and the Internet: Network Fundamentals, The Internet, The World Wide Web, Internet Protocols
Hafta 11: Artificial Intelligence: Intelligence and Machines, Perception, Reasoning, Additional Areas of Research, Artificial Neural Networks, Robotics, Considering the Consequences
Hafta 13: Informatics and Internet Management in Turkey, Internet and Law, Legal Regulations about IT Law
resources: Invitation to Computer Science, by Schneider, G. Michael, and Judith Gersting, 5th Edition, Nelson Education, ISBN: 0324788592
Computer Science: An Overview, by Glenn Brookshear, 11th Edition, Pearson, ISBN: 0321524039
Computing Essentials 2012 Complete Edition. ISBN: 0071221077 - By: Timothy J. O'leary, Linda I.O'leary. McGraw-Hill.

courses.name: Introduction to Programming II
courses.purpose: This course aims to establish a deep understanding of the principles of object-oriented programming using the selected programming language. Java is used throughout the course.
courses.description: The principles of object oriented programming.
learning_outcomes: At the end of this course successful students will understand the principles of object oriented programming including encapsulation, inheritance and polymorphism.
They will be able to develop object oriented programs in order to solve problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Object Oriented Programming
Hafta 14: Introduction to Graphical User Interface with Java
resources: Thinking in Java (4th edition), B. Eckel
How to Think Like a Computer Scientist (jave version), A. B. Downey
Head First Java (2nd Edition), K. Sierra and B. Bates, O'Reilly Media, 2009
Java Tutorials, http://download.oracle.com/javase/tutorial/java/index.html
The Java API, http://download.oracle.com/javase/6/docs/api/

courses.name: Introduction to Programming Laboratory II
courses.purpose: This main aim of this course is to establish a deep understanding of the principles of object-oriented programming using the selected programming language practically. The Java programming language will be used throughout.
courses.description: The principles of object oriented programming.
learning_outcomes: At the end of this course successful students will understand the principles of object oriented programming including encapsulation, inheritance and polymorphism.
They will be able to develop object oriented programs in order to solve problems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Object Oriented Programming
Hafta 14: Introduction to Graphical User Interface with Java
resources: Thinking in Java (4th edition), B. Eckel
How to Think Like a Computer Scientist (jave version), A. B. Downey
Head First Java (2nd Edition), K. Sierra and B. Bates, O'Reilly Media, 2009
Java Tutorials, http://download.oracle.com/javase/tutorial/java/index.html
The Java API, http://download.oracle.com/javase/6/docs/api/

courses.name: Data Structures
courses.purpose: Providing solid foundations in the basic concepts of programming, choosing and designing data structures that are appropriate for a problem and comparing the considered algorithms.
courses.description: Space and time complexity, matrix storage, stack, queue, singly/doubly linked lists, binary trees, graphs
learning_outcomes: Understand fundemantals of data structures.
Determine space and time complexity of some basic algorithms.
Learn the basic concepts for recursive algorithms
Specialize in matrix storage(sparse, band, lower/upper triangular matrix)
Learn how to use some essential data structures such as stack, queue, singly/doubly linked list
Design the efficient algorithms in converting and evaluating infix,prefix,postfix expressions
Implement an array-based or a pointer-based linked list and discuss advantage and disadvantage of both implementations
Learn the basic concepts of binary tree and graph data structures
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Basic concepts for data structures
Hafta 2: Performance analysis, space and time complexity
Hafta 4: Lower/upper triangular, sparse and band matrices
Hafta 9: Linked list applications (stack, queue, hashtable)
resources: Horowitz E., Sahni S., Anderson S., Fundamentals of Data Structures in C, Computer Scince Press, New York, 1993
Horowitz E., Sahni S., Mehta D., Fundamentals of Data Structures in C++, Computer Scince Press, New York, 1995
Problem Solving and Program Design in C, 7th Edition. Jeri Hanly and Elliot Koffman, Pearson, 2013
Data Structures and Algorithm Analysis in C++. 4th Edition, Mark Allen Weiss, Pearson, 2014

courses.name: SOFTWARE LABORATORY I
courses.purpose: To enable the students in writing and executing sample programs using the concepts taught in Data Structures course.
courses.description: The laboratory includes 4 programming assignments. The students are expected to present a well-designed documentary report with their assignments. The utilization of compilers, package programs and recent IDEs (Integrated Development Environments) are included in this laboratory.
learning_outcomes: Student will be able to develop an application.
Student will be able to produce algorithmic solutions for given problems.
Student will be able to use any programming language for the application that he/she develops
Student will be able to write reports for the applications he/she develops.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Presentation of the first experiment
Hafta 5: Presentation of the second experiment
Hafta 6: Examples of stacks and queues
Hafta 7: Examples of arithmetical/mathematical expressions? representation
Hafta 8: Presentation of the third experiment
Hafta 9: Examples of linked list applications
Hafta 10: Examples of doubly linked lists
Hafta 11: Presentation of the fourth experiment
resources: Horowitz E., Sahni S. and Mehta D., Fundamentals of Data Structures in C++, Computer Science Press, 1995.
Deitel H.M. and Deitel P.J., How to Program C++ and Java, Prentice-Hall, 2001.
Problem Solving and Program Design in C, 7th Edition. Jeri Hanly and Elliot Koff-man, Pearson, 2013
Data Structures and Algorithm Analysis in C++. 4th Edition, Mark Allen Weiss, Pearson, 2014

courses.name: DISCRETE STRUCTURES
courses.purpose: The aim of the course is to provide the students with basic knowledge and skills in discrete mathematics needed in the IT field.
courses.description: Logic and Proofs, Relations, Functions, Counting Rules, Algorithms, Recurrence Relations, Graphs, Discrete Probability, Modular Arithmetic
learning_outcomes: Use logical methods and desribe proof techniques
Describe recurrence relations and use recurrence relations with algorithms
Classify graphs and describe main concepts related to structures of graphs
Apply the rules of modular arithmetic and number theory
Apply probabilistic rules on discrete structures
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Logic: Propositional logic, predicates and quantifiers, logical reasoning
Hafta 4: Recursion: Definition, solving recursive equations
Hafta 6: Counting: Pigeonhole principle, permutations and combinations
Hafta 8: Arithmetic modulo m, primes and greatest common divisors
Hafta 9: Introduction to discrete probability, events, conditional probability, Bayes? rule
Hafta 10: Random variables, expectation and important distributions
Hafta 11: Graph terminology and graph isomorphism
Hafta 12: Connectivity, Euler and Hamilton paths, planar graphs, graph coloring
Hafta 13: Matchings and maximum matchings in graphs
resources: Discrete Mathematics and Its Applications, 7th Edition, Kenneth H. Rosen.
Mathematics for Computer Science, Eric Lehman and Tom Leighton, 2004

courses.name: LOGIC DESIGN
courses.purpose: To teach the basic components, the functions, and the analysis and design methods of digital circuits at introductory level.
courses.description: Digital systems, number systems, binary codes, error detecting and error correcting codes. Boolean algebra, switching algebra, binary operations and Boolean functions. Minimization of Boolean functions. Combinational logic, logic gates, analysis of combinational circuits, circuit design with logical gates. Integrated circuits, design with MSI chipsets, ROM, PLA. Synchronous sequential circuits, memory elements, analysis and design procedures. Registers, counters, RAM. Asynchronous sequential circuits.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to define basic concepts of digital circuits.
Students will be able to analyse digital circuits.
Students will be able to design digital circuits.
Students will be able to optimize digital circuits
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Basic concepts, number systems, binary number system.
Hafta 2: Coding, error detecting codes, error correcting codes.
Hafta 7: Two-level and multi-level circuits, adders, substracters.
Hafta 8: Adder and substracter circuits, comparators, decoders, encoders, multiplexers, demultiplexers.
Hafta 9: Read only memory, programmable logic arrays.
Hafta 10: Synchronous sequential circuits, finite state automata, flip-flops.
Hafta 12: Synchronous sequential circuit design, case studies.
resources: Yarımağan Ü., Sayısal Devrelerde Mantıksal Tasarım, Bıçaklar Kitabevi, 2003.
Mano M.M., Digital Design, 3rd ed., Prentice Hall, 2001.

courses.name: LOGIC DESIGN LABORATORY
courses.purpose: To enable the students practicing digital circuit design using the fundamental concepts taught in Logic Design course.
courses.description: The laboratory includes practical assignments on logical gates, integrated circuits, properties and rules of Boolean algebra, combinational circuits, minimization of Boolean functions, code converters, multiplexers, magnitude comparators, parallel adders, flip-flops, sequential circuits, counters, registers, serial adders, and memory elements.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to define basic electronic compenents used in digital circuits.
Students will be able to analyse digital circuits.
Students will be able to design digital circuits.
Students will be able to physically implement digital circuits.
resources: Yarımağan Ü., Sayısal Devrelerde Mantıksal Tasarım, Bıçaklar Kitabevi, 2003.
Mano M.M., Digital Design, 3rd ed., Prentice Hall, 2001.

courses.name: Atatürk's Princ. And The History of The Revol. I
ALGORITHMS
courses.purpose: The goal of this course is to teach student how to develop algorithms in order to solve the complex problems in the most efficient way.
courses.description: Analysis of algorithms, sorting, searching, graph algorithms, and string processing.
learning_outcomes: The students are expected to develop a foundational understanding and knowledge of key concepts that underly important algorithms in use on computers today.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Elementary Search Algorithms, Binary Search Trees
resources: Algorithms, 4th Edition, R. Sedgewick and K. Wayne, Addison-Wesley Professional, 2011
1. Horowitz E., Sahni S., Anderson S., Fundamentals of Data Structures in C, Computer Scince Press, New York, 1993
2. Horowitz E., Sahni S., Mehta D., Fundamentals of Data Structures in C++, Computer Scince Press, New York, 1995

courses.name: SOFTWARE LABORATORY II
courses.purpose: The goal of this course is to teach student how to develop and apply algorithms in order to solve the complex problems in the most efficient way.
courses.description: Analysis of algorithms, sorting, searching, graph algorithms, and string processing.
learning_outcomes: The students are expected to practice about key concepts that underly important algorithms in use on computers today.
weekly_topics: Hafta 4: Elementary Search Algorithms, Binary Search Trees
resources: Algorithms, 4th Edition, R. Sedgewick and K. Wayne, Addison-Wesley Professional, 2011

courses.name: COMPUTER ORGANIZATION
courses.purpose: The subject matter of this course is to teach the fundamentals of computer architecture at an introductory level.
courses.description: CPU performance comparison; digital building blocks of computer architecture; machine code, instruction concept, microprogramming concept, addressing modes; single-cycle and pipelined architectures; memory organization; mass storage devices; I/O devices.
learning_outcomes: After completing the course, the students will gain deep understanding about basic mathematical and logical concepts in computer organization.
They will be ready for the systems courses such as systems programming, operating systems.
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Computer Architecture: MIPS Instruction Set and Registers
Hafta 5: Computer Architecture: Procedure Calls, Addressing
Hafta 8: Microarchitecture: Single-Cycle MIPS Processor Implementation
Hafta 12: Memory Systems: Memory System Performance Analysis, Caches
Hafta 13: Memory Systems: Memory System Performance Analysis, Caches
Hafta 14: Memory Systems: Virtual memory, paging, TLB
resources: Digital Design and Computer Architecture, D. Harris, S. Harris, Morgan Kaufmann, 2012.

courses.name: Introduction to Electronic Circ. and Syst.
Atatürk's Princ. And The History of The Revol. II
PROGRAMMING LANGUAGES
courses.purpose: The subject matter of this course is to provide a programmer’s perspective on how computer systems execute programs and store data.
courses.description: syntax semantics names bindings type checking subprograms
learning_outcomes: After completing the course, the students will gain an analytical and comparative look at programming languages, their implementations,
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: Names, bindings, type checking and scopes
resources: Concepts of Programming Languages, Robert W. Sebesta, Pearson, Tenth Edition, 2012

courses.name: SYSTEM PROGRAMMING
courses.purpose: The subject matter of this course is to provide a programmer’s perspective on how computer systems execute programs and store data.
courses.description: Data representation, computer arithmetic, assembly language, code optimization, memory organization and management, linking, exceptional control flow, virtual memory, and system-level I/O.
learning_outcomes: After completing the course, the students will gain deep understanding of inner workings of computer systems
become better programmers
have hand-on experiences via a set of programming assignments
read and discuss some research papers from the current literature
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Integer representations and integer arithmetic
Hafta 5: Assembly: Control structures and procedures
resources: Computer Systems: A Programmers Perspective, 2/E (CS:APP2e), Randal E. Bryant and David R. O'Hallaron, Prentice Hall, 2011

courses.name: DATA MANAGEMENT
courses.purpose: To develop an understanding and practice for fundamental data management systems with a focus on relational data.
courses.description: Database architectures, relational data model, database design, SQL, query processing, complex and big data storage, tree and hash based indexing, spatial and multidimensional index structures.
learning_outcomes: Designing relational databases using ER-diagrams
Performing all levels of queries using SQL
Understanding the concept and types of files, organization and access of files
Geting familar with physical storage structures
Comparing the performance of index types, tree and hash structured file organization, and external sorting.
Understanding the diferent storage and indexing schemes for complex and spatial data
weekly_topics: Hafta 1: Introduction to Data Management and Database Architecture
Hafta 2: Database Design Using The Entity-Relationship Model
Hafta 14: Query Processing (join algorithms, external sorting)
resources: Ramakrishnan R. ve Gehrke J., Database Management Systems, 2nd ed., McGraw-Hill Education, 1999.
Hector Garcia-Molina, Jeffrey D. Ullman and Jennifer D. Widom. Database Systems The Complete Book. Pearson 2008
Avi Silberschatz, Henry J. Korth, S. Sudarshan: Database System Concepts, Seventh Edition. McGraw-Hill Book Company 2020, ISBN 9780078022159

courses.name: OPERATING SYSTEMS
courses.purpose: To teach basic concepts of operating systems and operating systems infrastructure.
courses.description: Basic operating systems concepts, Input/Output subsystem, Process Management, Process synchronization, Memory management, File management, Distributed processing and network infrastructure, Operating systems security.
learning_outcomes: Students will be able to define basic concepts of operating systems
Students will be able to understand operating systems infrastructure and interpret operations in sub layers of operating systems
Students will obtain a basic level of experience in operating systems programming.
resources: Operating System Design, Avi Silberschatz, Peter Baer Galvin, Greg Gagne, 8th edition, John Wiley & Sons, Inc.
Modern Operating Systems, Andrew Tanenbaum, 3rd edition, Pearson Education.
Operating Systems: Principles and Practice. Anderson, Thomas, and Michael Dahlin. Recursive books, 2014
Bilgisayar İşletim Sistemleri, Ali Saatçi, 2. Baskı, Bıçaklar Kitabevi

courses.name: SOFTWARE ENGINEERING
courses.purpose: To teach the basic concepts and processes of software engineering including the management, the quality assurance, and the support of software systems.
courses.description: Basic concepts of software engineering. Types of computer systems and the software as a part of them. The relation of software engineering to the systems engineering. The scope of software engineering: Software development (analysis, design, coding, and test), software engineering management, software configuration management, software engineering processes, software engineering tools and methods, and software quality assurance. Software metrics and software cost estimation. Cost of software quality. Software development process models and process reference models.
learning_outcomes: To have general knowledge about software engineering concepts
weekly_topics: Hafta 2: Prj. Mgt, Prj. Planning & Risk Mgt.
resources: Sommerville I., Software Engineering, 9th ed., Addison-Wesley Professional, 2011.
Pilone D. ve Pitman N., UML 2.0 in a Nutshell, 2nd ed., O'Reilly Media, 2005.

courses.name: Software Eng. Lab.
courses.purpose: To experiment the development of middle-size software systems by following basic software engineering practices.
courses.description: Engineering middle-size software applications from the scratch by using both management and development practices. Initial requirements of software projects and planning its development. Practicing requirements analysis, architectural design, and detailed design of software systems in accordance to a pre-defined software development life-cycle (based on Open Unified Process), and documenting the outputs of these activities in pre-defined formats (as recommended by IEEE). The students are required to show a working prototype of the software architecture by coding several critical requirements of the system at the end of the laboratory. The use of J2EE technologies is intended during the design and implementation of the software system.
learning_outcomes: To have experience about the development of middle-size software systems by following basic software engineering practices
weekly_topics: Hafta 3: UML Modeling (Context, Use Case, Activity D.)
Hafta 4: Example Requirements Modeling with UML
Hafta 5: Example Requirements Modeling with UML
Hafta 6: UML Modeling (Package, Component, Deployment D.)
Hafta 7: Example Arch. & HL Design Modeling with UML
Hafta 8: UML Modeling (Statechart, Class, Sequence D.) - 1
Hafta 9: UML Modeling (Statechart, Class, Sequence D.) - 2
Hafta 10: Example Design & Impl. Modeling with UML
resources: Sommerville I., Software Engineering, 9th ed., Addison-Wesley Professional, 2011.
Pilone D. and Pitman N., UML 2.0 in a Nutshell, 2nd ed., O'Reilly Media, 2005.
